items.title: U.S. strikes Iran after Trump says Tehran committed "foolish violation" of ceasefire
items.standfirst: The U.S. struck back at Iran Friday after an Iranian drone attack on a cargo ship Thursday in the Strait of Hormuz. CENTCOM said U.S. aircraft hit Iran's missile and drone storage locations. Nikole Killion reports.
items.content: The U.S. struck back at Iran Friday after an Iranian drone attack on a cargo ship Thursday in the Strait of Hormuz. CENTCOM said U.S. aircraft hit Iran's missile and drone storage locations. Nikole Killion reports.

items.title: US conducts strikes on Iran after attack on cargo ship
items.standfirst: US Central Command says it has struck missile and drone storage facilities and coastal radar positions.
items.content: US Central Command says it has struck missile and drone storage facilities and coastal radar positions.

items.title: JD Vance says, "Violence will be met with violence" after Iran strikes
items.standfirst: The U.S. launched a new round of air strikes one day after an Iranian attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command said the strikes targeted missile and drone storage locations as well as coastal radar sites. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has more.
items.content: The U.S. launched a new round of air strikes one day after an Iranian attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command said the strikes targeted missile and drone storage locations as well as coastal radar sites. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has more.

items.title: Early details about U.S. retaliatory strikes on Iran
items.standfirst: The U.S. has conducted new airstrikes on Iran after the country attacked a ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, the U.S. military says. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more details.
items.content: The U.S. has conducted new airstrikes on Iran after the country attacked a ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, the U.S. military says. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more details.

items.title: US says it struck Iran targets after attack on cargo ship in the strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: Strikes against military facilities were in response to drone attack a day earlier on a cargo vesselUS strikes Iran – latest updatesThe US has struck Iran in a tit-for-tat response to a drone strike on a cargo ship, as the ceasefire between the US and Iran that reopened the strait of Hormuz undergoes its greatest test yet.The US strikes targeted multiple missile and drone facilities in Iran near the strait of Hormuz and on Qeshm Island on Friday in what appeared to be a limited strike meant to respond to Iran’s attack on a Singapore-flagged cargo ship without escalating the conflict. Continue reading...
items.content: Strikes against military facilities were in response to drone attack a day earlier on a cargo vesselUS strikes Iran – latest updatesThe US has struck Iran in a tit-for-tat response to a drone strike on a cargo ship, as the ceasefire between the US and Iran that reopened the strait of Hormuz undergoes its greatest test yet.The US strikes targeted multiple missile and drone facilities in Iran near the strait of Hormuz and on Qeshm Island on Friday in what appeared to be a limited strike meant to respond to Iran’s attack on a Singapore-flagged cargo ship without escalating the conflict. Continue reading...

items.title: U.S. targets missile, drone storage locations in Iran, CENTCOM says
items.standfirst: The U.S. fired retaliatory strikes against Iran on Friday. CENTCOM said the strikes targeted missile and drone storage locations as well as coastal radar sites. The launch is in direct response to an Iranian attack on a Singapore-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday. CBS News' Kathryn Watson reports.
items.content: The U.S. fired retaliatory strikes against Iran on Friday. CENTCOM said the strikes targeted missile and drone storage locations as well as coastal radar sites. The launch is in direct response to an Iranian attack on a Singapore-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday. CBS News' Kathryn Watson reports.

items.title: U.S. strikes targets in Iran after drone attack on cargo ship
items.standfirst: The U.S. military says it hit Iranian targets over Iran's drone attack on a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, marking the first American strikes on Iran since the two countries formally agreed to extend a ceasefire last week.
items.content: The U.S. military says it hit Iranian targets over Iran's drone attack on a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz, marking the first American strikes on Iran since the two countries formally agreed to extend a ceasefire last week.

items.title: Middle East War: US strikes Iran after Trump says ceasefire was violated
items.standfirst: The US struck Iran on Friday to respond to a drone attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, a provocation that US President Donald Trump said violated the ceasefire. The British military said Thursday that a vessel was hit by a projectile off Oman. This comes at a fragile time for the US and Iranian negotiations over interim peace deal. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: The US struck Iran on Friday to respond to a drone attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, a provocation that US President Donald Trump said violated the ceasefire. The British military said Thursday that a vessel was hit by a projectile off Oman. This comes at a fragile time for the US and Iranian negotiations over interim peace deal. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.standfirst: US president calls it ‘foolish violation’ of ceasefire agreement after drone damaged ship’s upper deckUS politics live – latest updatesDonald Trump on Friday blamed Iran for carrying out a drone strike on a cargo ship in the strait of Hormuz, calling it a “foolish violation” of the ceasefire agreement with the US.One drone damaged the upper deck of the ship but the ship was able to proceed, the US president said. The US shot down three other drones aimed at the ship, he said. Continue reading...

items.title: Hormuz traffic flows despite ship attack as Trump accuses Iran of ‘foolish’ ceasefire breach
items.standfirst: A trickle of marine traffic was passing through the Strait of Hormuz on Friday despite an attack on a ship in the vital waterway a day earlier, as Iran reiterated its warnings for ships to follow a Tehran-approved route
items.content: A trickle of marine traffic was passing through the Strait of Hormuz on Friday despite an attack on a ship in the vital waterway a day earlier, as Iran reiterated its warnings for ships to follow a Tehran-approved route

items.title: Iran strikes vessel in Strait of Hormuz amid debate over "transit fees"
items.standfirst: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps struck a cargo ship near Oman on Thursday, further complicating the Iran-U.S. negotiations.
items.content: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps struck a cargo ship near Oman on Thursday, further complicating the Iran-U.S. negotiations.

items.title: UN agency pauses ship evacuations through strait of Hormuz after vessel struck
items.standfirst: International Maritime Organization says safety guarantees must be confirmed before ships can move againA United Nations agency has paused the evacuation of ships through the strait of Hormuz after the British military said a vessel was hit by a projectile off the coast of Oman following the passage of several tankers that used a route backed by the UN.The head of the UN’s International Maritime Organization said on Thursday that the plan to move stranded ships out of the Persian Gulf through the strait would be on hold until the agency could confirm safety guarantees for the ships on the evacuation list and in the region. Continue reading...
items.content: International Maritime Organization says safety guarantees must be confirmed before ships can move againA United Nations agency has paused the evacuation of ships through the strait of Hormuz after the British military said a vessel was hit by a projectile off the coast of Oman following the passage of several tankers that used a route backed by the UN.The head of the UN’s International Maritime Organization said on Thursday that the plan to move stranded ships out of the Persian Gulf through the strait would be on hold until the agency could confirm safety guarantees for the ships on the evacuation list and in the region. clusters.title: Advocates warn of wide-ranging implications of US Supreme Court TPS ruling
clusters.neutral_headline: Advocates warn of wide-ranging implications of US Supreme Court TPS ruling
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, BBC World

items.title: Advocates warn of wide-ranging implications of US Supreme Court TPS ruling
items.standfirst: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haiti and Syria, threatening family separation say advocates.
items.content: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haiti and Syria, threatening family separation say advocates.

items.title: Over a million immigrants risk deportation from US after landmark decision
items.standfirst: The US Supreme Court has handed the Trump Administration another win on its policies aggressively cracking down on immigration and allowing mass deportations. In the conservative majority's 6-3 decision -the justices ruled that Washington can lift the temporary protected status of hundreds of thousands of Haitian and Syrian migrants in the United States. That decision paves the way for those TPS holders to be sent back to their home countries.
items.content: The US Supreme Court has handed the Trump Administration another win on its policies aggressively cracking down on immigration and allowing mass deportations. In the conservative majority's 6-3 decision -the justices ruled that Washington can lift the temporary protected status of hundreds of thousands of Haitian and Syrian migrants in the United States. That decision paves the way for those TPS holders to be sent back to their home countries.

items.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
items.standfirst: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.
items.content: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, France24, NPR News

items.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified documents
items.standfirst: Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say.
items.content: Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say.

items.title: Former Trump national security adviser Bolton pleads guilty in classified files case
items.standfirst: John Bolton, a former national security adviser to US President Donald Trump who has since become one of his most vocal critics, pleaded guilty Friday to withholding classified documents related to national defence.
items.content: John Bolton, a former national security adviser to US President Donald Trump who has since become one of his most vocal critics, pleaded guilty Friday to withholding classified documents related to national defence.

items.title: John Bolton, former Trump national security adviser, pleads guilty in classified documents case
items.standfirst: Bolton pleaded guilty to one count of retaining national defense information while he was a national security adviser during President Trump's first term, saying: "And I am sorry for it."
items.content: Bolton pleaded guilty to one count of retaining national defense information while he was a national security adviser during President Trump's first term, saying: "And I am sorry for it."(Image credit: Alex Kent)

clusters.title: Race to find survivors as Venezuela's earthquake death toll soars
clusters.neutral_headline: Race to find survivors as Venezuela's earthquake death toll soars
bullet_summary: In Venezuela, the confirmed death toll is rapidly growing as rescue teams race to find survivors trapped in the rubble after two devastating earthquakes
Reported by CBS News, NBC World

items.title: Race to find survivors as Venezuela's earthquake death toll soars
items.standfirst: The twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 900 people, and that toll is likely to keep rising as frantic rescue and recovery operations ramp up.
items.content: The twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 900 people, and that toll is likely to keep rising as frantic rescue and recovery operations ramp up.

items.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll tops 900 as rescuers race to find survivors
items.standfirst: The death toll of the Venezuelan earthquakes soared past 900 on Friday. But there is still time - one more day - in what's considered to be the "golden" window for finding survivors. Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the latest.
items.content: The death toll of the Venezuelan earthquakes soared past 900 on Friday. But there is still time - one more day - in what's considered to be the "golden" window for finding survivors. Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the latest.

items.title: What's needed on the ground in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes
items.standfirst: Rescue teams in Venezuela are racing to dig through the rubble to locate thousands of people who are still missing after two earthquakes rattled the country. Fabio Biolchini, the head of operations for Latin America at Doctors Without Borders, joins "The Daily Report" with more on the immediate needs on the ground.
items.content: Rescue teams in Venezuela are racing to dig through the rubble to locate thousands of people who are still missing after two earthquakes rattled the country. Fabio Biolchini, the head of operations for Latin America at Doctors Without Borders, joins "The Daily Report" with more on the immediate needs on the ground.

items.title: Emergency crews in Venezuela race to find survivors after earthquakes
items.standfirst: In Venezuela, the confirmed death toll is rapidly growing as rescue teams race to find survivors trapped in the rubble after two devastating earthquakes. Officials report at least 920 are dead in the aftermath, and over 3,300 are injured. CBS News correspondent Cristian Benavides reports.
items.content: In Venezuela, the confirmed death toll is rapidly growing as rescue teams race to find survivors trapped in the rubble after two devastating earthquakes. Officials report at least 920 are dead in the aftermath, and over 3,300 are injured. CBS News correspondent Cristian Benavides reports.

items.title: Venezuelans hurry through quake rubble in search for thousands of survivors
items.standfirst: Massive search and rescue operations are underway in Venezuela on Friday as civilians and emergency workers rushed to find those trapped beneath the rubble more than a day after devastating twin earthquakes killed over 589 people.
items.content: Massive search and rescue operations are underway in Venezuela on Friday as civilians and emergency workers rushed to find those trapped beneath the rubble more than a day after devastating twin earthquakes killed over 589 people.

items.content: Dembele's ⁠first-half hat-trick helps France to a 4-1 win over second-string Norway ⁠to finish top of Group I.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Senegal crush 10-man Iraq to remain in hunt for knockout spot
items.standfirst: Senegal kept their hopes of qualifying for the World Cup knockouts alive with a thumping 5-0 win over Iraq on Friday as they finished third in Group I behind France and Norway. The Lions of Teranga remain in the hunt for one of the last eight spots in the ⁠round of 32, while Iraq's tournament is over.
items.content: Senegal kept their hopes of qualifying for the World Cup knockouts alive with a thumping 5-0 win over Iraq on Friday as they finished third in Group I behind France and Norway. The Lions of Teranga remain in the hunt for one of the last eight spots in the ⁠round of 32, while Iraq's tournament is over.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Dembélé scores sensational hat-trick as France beat Norway to top group
items.standfirst: Ballon d'Or winner Ousmane Dembélé scored a magnificent first-half hat-trick, his first with Les Bleus, as France beat a second-string Norway 4-1 on Friday to seal top spot in Group I. Star striker Erling Haaland was one of several players rested by the Norway coach ahead of the World Cup knockouts stage.
items.content: Ballon d'Or winner Ousmane Dembélé scored a magnificent first-half hat-trick, his first with Les Bleus, as France beat a second-string Norway 4-1 on Friday to seal top spot in Group I. Star striker Erling Haaland was one of several players rested by the Norway coach ahead of the World Cup knockouts stage.

items.title: France top World Cup group as Dembélé's first-half treble downs second-string Norway
items.standfirst: Ballon ⁠d'Or ⁠winner Ousmane Dembélé scored three goals in just 25 minutes as France defeated Norway's reserves 4-1 to seal top spot in Group I on Friday, while Senegal kept their hopes of a World Cup knockouts spot alive with a crushing victory over Iraq. Read our liveblog to see how the matches unfolded.
items.content: Ballon ⁠d'Or ⁠winner Ousmane Dembélé scored three goals in just 25 minutes as France defeated Norway's reserves 4-1 to seal top spot in Group I on Friday, while Senegal kept their hopes of a World Cup knockouts spot alive with a crushing victory over Iraq. clusters.neutral_headline: Burkina Faso cuts diplomatic ties with former colonial ruler France
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Al Jazeera

items.title: Burkina Faso ruling junta cuts diplomatic ties with ex-ruler France
items.standfirst: Burkina Faso's military junta on Friday cut diplomatic ties with former colonial power France, accusing Paris of acting against its interests and backing subversive networks and militants in the Sahel. The move deepens the ruling authorities' break with Western allies since Captain Ibrahim Traore seized power in a 2022 coup.
items.content: Burkina Faso's military junta on Friday cut diplomatic ties with former colonial power France, accusing Paris of acting against its interests and backing subversive networks and militants in the Sahel. The move deepens the ruling authorities' break with Western allies since Captain Ibrahim Traore seized power in a 2022 coup.

clusters.neutral_headline: Venezuela’s Powerful Earthquakes Were a Rare ‘Seismic Doublet’
bullet_summary: Reported by Wired, NBC World

items.title: Venezuela’s Powerful Earthquakes Were a Rare ‘Seismic Doublet’
items.standfirst: The country was hit hard by a pair of quakes that happened in quick succession and were likely driven by stress being transferred from one part of the fault that runs through the country to another.
items.content: The country was hit hard by a pair of quakes that happened in quick succession and were likely driven by stress being transferred from one part of the fault that runs through the country to another.

items.title: What to know about ‘doublet’ earthquakes like the destructive pair that hit Venezuela
items.standfirst: Venezuela’s pair of earthquakes is considered a doublet sequence. It’s not rare to have two quakes in short succession, but in this case, that most likely led to more destruction.
items.content: Venezuela’s pair of earthquakes is considered a doublet sequence. It’s not rare to have two quakes in short succession, but in this case, that most likely led to more destruction.
